axis: SHA-1 default gate-hook migration to perl-less compatible precedence pattern
trigger: post-runner-2 hexa-runner:23021b99 noise — 4x shasum-not-found per invocation

sites_audited.fires_on: every Claude Code post-edit gate hook on host

sites_audited.purpose: ph_cmd — git remote origin 12-hex slot key for project_hash
sites_audited.fires_on: every Claude Code post-edit gate hook on host
every Claude Code post-bash gate hook on host
every Claude Code post-bash gate hook on host

sites_audited.purpose: _vp_sha1 — path 12-hex slot key for python VP block tmp file
sites_audited.fires_on: lint pass that processes ```python fenced blocks

sites_migrated.before: printf '%s' "$TERM_SESSION_ID" 2>/dev/null | /usr/bin/shasum -a 1 2>/dev/null | cut -c1-8  AND  git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null | /usr/bin/shasum -a 1 2>/dev/null | cut -c1-12
sites_migrated.after: printf '%s' "$TERM_SESSION_ID" 2>/dev/null | { sha256sum 2>/dev/null || /usr/bin/shasum -a 1 2>/dev/null; } | cut -c1-8  AND  git remote get-url origin 2>/dev/null | { sha256sum 2>/dev/null || /usr/bin/shasum -a 1 2>/dev/null; } | cut -c1-12
sites_migrated.comment_added: // w-runner-3: precedence-swap to perl-less-container-compatible — sha256sum primary, shasum -a 1 fallback. Slot key (8/12 hex), not crypto. One-time cache miss on key regen.
// w-runner-3: precedence-swap to perl-less-container-compatible — sha256sum primary, shasum -a 1 fallback. Path slot key (12 hex), not crypto. One-time cache miss on key regen.

deferred_sites_resolved_in_this_cycle: post_edit.hexa:50 ph_cmd (was deferred in runner-2 — migrated here on the same diff hunk as :48)
post_bash.hexa:50 ph_cmd (mirror — migrated here on the same diff hunk as :48)

validation.noise_unchanged_root_cause: Gate-hook edits do NOT eliminate the 4 noise lines. They are correct on their own merits (perl-less-container compat for any future in-container gate-hook execution + portable hash for slot keys), but the noise originates elsewhere.

surprise_finding.summary: The 4 'shasum: not found' lines do NOT come from any gate hook. They come from the BAKED Linux ELF /usr/local/bin/hexa inside the hexa-runner:23021b99 image, which still contains pre-runner-2 strings.
evidence: Direct probe: `docker cp hexa-exec:/usr/local/bin/hexa /tmp/_hexa_elf && strings /tmp/_hexa_elf | grep shasum` returns:
')" | shasum -a 256 | cut -c1-16 | tr -d '\n'        (matches main.hexa:1016 source_key — PRE-runner-2 form)
\n'; cat) | shasum -a 256 | cut -c1-64 | tr -d '\n'  (matches main.hexa:1217 src_fingerprint_deep — PRE-runner-2 form)

Both strings lack the `{ sha256sum 2>/dev/null || shasum -a 256; }` group-pipe that runner-2 added to source. Conclusion: the ELF inside hexa-runner:23021b99 was compiled from a tree that pre-dates the runner-2 sha256sum precedence swap.

Container has /usr/bin/sha256sum AND /usr/bin/perl (perl-base only, NOT libdigest-sha-perl) — so /usr/bin/shasum is the only thing missing. The post-runner-2 'remove perl' Dockerfile change in fact only removed libdigest-sha-perl (which carries /usr/bin/shasum); /usr/bin/perl itself is dragged in by some other dep (perl-base).
Tested cwd inside container (-w /tmp, no host bind-mount cwd): noise still fires 4x. Confirms origin is the binary itself, not any host-side .hexa script.

The ELF has 0 references to gate/post_edit.hexa, gate/post_bash.hexa, gate/lint.hexa — those gate hooks are Claude Code HOST hooks, fired by Claude Code after a tool result, NOT during a `hexa <file>` invocation. So gate hooks were never going to be on the noise path.
surprise_finding.implication: The cycle's stated goal (eliminate 4x noise via gate-hook migration) was based on an incorrect attribution. The real fix requires rebuilding /usr/local/bin/hexa from current source (which carries the runner-2 sha256sum precedence) and re-baking the runner image. Per cycle constraints (NO rebuild), this was not done.

side_effects: TSID/path/project_hash cache slots in gate hook rows will regenerate to SHA-256-prefix keys on first gate-hook fire after this change. One-time cache miss expected — pattern_key dedup may briefly inflate before stabilizing.
noise_remediation_recommendation_for_fol: omega-runner-4: rebuild /usr/local/bin/hexa from current hexa-lang source and re-bake hexa-runner image. After rebuild, expected shasum-not-found count: 0 (sha256sum primary always wins inside container). No additional source edits needed.
rollback: git checkout HEAD -- gate/post_edit.hexa gate/post_bash.hexa gate/lint.hexa
